first 10 years struggle. With the
exception of the iron and steel
workers, and possibly one or two
other trades, all the national or
CARNATIONS will report increases in
memberships, in the face of the bit
terest opposition, which, in some
cases. are truly remarkable.


For example, the printers have
been subjected to the most severe
contest, which cost them upward
of $5,000,000,, but they came
through with an Increase in mem
bers, the shorter work day and a
general increase in wages rang
ing from 25 per cent upward.


The miners were forced to spend
the next highest sum in battling
against the open shop. 1t cost
them over 4,000,000-but here is
the unparalleled result: Hours Of
labor have been reduced to eight
per day. wages of the poorest paid
have advanced so to 100 per cent,
and their membership has in
creased enormously.
